kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Intergraph Smart Services</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>0 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Intergraph solutions cover all aspects of the design, build, and operate life cycle, and the integration of these applications provides even greater value than the individual components alone. Given the complexity and scale of the projects that incorporate many different elements, Intergraph helps you successfully implement the right solutions using a three-stage process, with the key resources being:</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>1</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>1. A business analyst consults with the various departments of your organization to define the business issues that need to be resolved. The analyst works with you to prioritize your requirements according to greatest business benefit, and develops a roadmap for the introduction of solutions.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>2 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>2. A solution architect takes the requirements and roadmap developed by the business analyst and constructs a solution that meets your requirements in accordance with the roadmap.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>3 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>3. A project manager builds the project plan from an agreed task list and then allocates resources from Intergraph, contractors, and your organization to tasks in the project plan.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Our goal is always to ensure that the solutions being deployed truly meet your requirements - delivered on time and on budget.</a:t></a:r></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:rPr><a:pPr/><a:t>12</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:endParaRPr></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="819601660"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide15.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="419100" y="703263"/><a:ext cx="6248400" cy="3514725"/></a:xfrm></p:spPr></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="932785"><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>0 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>SDV helps you significantly reduce the time and costs associated with ensuring the quality of information,</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> whether that data is coming PMCs, EPCs, or legacy systems.  Y</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>ou have complete control of how supplied data – such as equipment lists, cable schedules, valve lists, etc. – are imported from multiple data sources</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="959369"/><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>1- </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Once</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> imported, t</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>he data elements can be mapped against the data structures of the target system. This means that you can validate your input data against the data structure of your target system simply by creating the mapping! A great time savings.</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>  </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Subject matter experts can also easily set up additional rules without any programming expertise</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> level required</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>.</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Validation rules could include things like:</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr marL="179882" indent="-179882"><a:buFont typeface="Arial" charset="0"/><a:buChar char="•"/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Checking that an item exists… Does Project 401-A exist in the target system?</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr marL="179882" indent="-179882" defTabSz="959369"><a:buFont typeface="Arial" charset="0"/><a:buChar char="•"/><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Confirming mandatory fields… A tag classification is required for each tag.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr marL="179882" indent="-179882" defTabSz="959369"><a:buFont typeface="Arial" charset="0"/><a:buChar char="•"/><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Ensuring relationship cardinality is maintained… A tag can only belong to one Area.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr marL="179882" indent="-179882" defTabSz="959369"><a:buFont typeface="Arial" charset="0"/><a:buChar char="•"/><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Verifying pick list values… Does the tag classification match one of the values in the defined list?</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="959369"><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>2- </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>In</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> the validation step, t</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>he</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> r</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>ules are applied to check the quality of the data.</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> Transformation rules can also be applied for things like converting data formats, for example.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="959369"/><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>3 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>A</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> quality r</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>eport is generated for each job</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>.  Data that fails validation can be corrected or sent back to the originator for remediation. A</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>ll results from testing are captured</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> and f</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>ull auditable traceability is provided of when and what testing has been performed, by whom, as well as the results of the testing. </a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>4 – Once approved, the dataset is exported and loaded into the Target System.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>In a variety of ways,</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> depending on the capabilities of the target system.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:pPr><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:fld id="{42B1CE3C-A4F0-0D46-A3E6-4BC4EA533F3C}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-US" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:rPr><a:pPr><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:t>19</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:endParaRPr></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="4062642205"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Requirement for final tool handover</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1100" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr lvl="0"/><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>We’re also seeing more and more scenarios where Owners are not only requesting periodic and incremental handovers from their EPCs but where they also want to obtain a copy of the integrated tool databases at the end of the project. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1100" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr lvl="0"/><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>We therefore will now looking at a solution for two requirements:</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1100" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr lvl="1"/><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>We’ll see how both, the EPC and the Owner Operator can benefit in terms of information handover when both sides are using SmartPlant Enterprise in order to periodically receive, validate and consolidate all these different information sources</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1100" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr lvl="1"/><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>And we will see how easy it becomes for the Owner Operator to also benefit from the design tools</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> where the tools may be handed over at the end of a project and simply re-registered on the target SPF/SPO system without the need to re-publish from the tools. After re-registration on the target system the Smart Plant design tools will be behave as if the data transferred via SPO Handover to the target system were published locally.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1100" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr lvl="0"/><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1100" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"/><a:t>24</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="263723359"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>0 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Intergraph solutions cover all aspects of the design, build, and operate life cycle, and the integration of these applications provides even greater value than the individual components alone. Given the complexity and scale of the projects that incorporate many different elements, Intergraph helps you successfully implement the right solutions using a three-stage process, with the key resources being:</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>1</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>1. A business analyst consults with the various departments of your organization to define the business issues that need to be resolved. The analyst works with you to prioritize your requirements according to greatest business benefit, and develops a roadmap for the introduction of solutions.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>2 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>2. A solution architect takes the requirements and roadmap developed by the business analyst and constructs a solution that meets your requirements in accordance with the roadmap.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" b="1" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>3 – </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>3. A project manager builds the project plan from an agreed task list and then allocates resources from Intergraph, contractors, and your organization to tasks in the project plan.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Our goal is always to ensure that the solutions being deployed truly meet your requirements - delivered on time and on budget.</a:t></a:r></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:rPr><a:pPr/><a:t>25</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:endParaRPr></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="28760948"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide19.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Reference back to ARC study and</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> ask potential customer how much 1.5% of their annual sales are and what actions they would take </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>to keep it.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"/><a:t>26</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="1254505470"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide2.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>But before we start, let us define the term Project Information Handover</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> for this webinar. In the following 20 minutes, when we talk about project information handover we talk about the final handover between contractors and Owner Operators. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>The most important thing is for all of us to understand that we are not talking about a technology challenge but about a commercial and contractual issue. Today we are going to look at the common handover challenges and which</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> technology is available to address those. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>During Handover it is critical to gather information for completion/commissioning</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>, </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>preparation for operations (such as spare-parts evaluation, preparation of training materials and system operating manuals etc.) and for start-up, operations and maintenance. This needs to happen as early as possible in order to de-risk the project schedule which minimizes the risk for delayed revenue.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>These information is needed to have smarter and more intelligent maintenance and inspection plans (RCM, RBI etc.) in order to minimize health &amp; safety risks and to reduce operating</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> expenditure. These information are also critical to </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>manage change on the operating plant and demonstrate compliance to regulatory authorities. As well as for</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> planning</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> shutdowns, turnarounds, debottlenecking and revamps etc. </a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>On the other hand we have some important project factors that need to be considered when it comes to collecting and distributing the required information. Who in the project is responsible for what; e.g. what data will be produced by which EPC? How can this information be validated against project information </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" err="1" smtClean="0"/><a:t>specifiations</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>?</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>How can you easily capture and reuse the information provided?</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" b="0" i="0" u="none" strike="noStrike" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" b="0" i="0" u="none" strike="noStrike" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>So Joris, saying all this, can you explain as a bit more detailed how handover typical works and what a typical handover timeline </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" b="0" i="0" u="none" strike="noStrike" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" err="1" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>wille</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" b="0" i="0" u="none" strike="noStrike" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> be?</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="de-DE" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="de-DE" sz="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"/><a:t>2</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="3742633745"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide3.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="38914" name="Rectangle 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1" noChangeArrowheads="1" noTextEdit="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr><a:ln/></p:spPr></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="38915" name="Rectangle 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noChangeArrowheads="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr><a:noFill/><a:ln/><a:extLst><a:ext uri="{909E8E84-426E-40DD-AFC4-6F175D3DCCD1}"><a14:hiddenFill xmlns:a14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/drawing/2010/main"><a:solidFill><a:srgbClr val="FFFFFF"/></a:solidFill></a14:hiddenFill></a:ext><a:ext uri="{91240B29-F687-4F45-9708-019B960494DF}"><a14:hiddenLine xmlns:a14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/drawing/2010/main" w="9525"><a:solidFill><a:srgbClr val="000000"/></a:solidFill><a:miter lim="800000"/><a:headEnd/><a:tailEnd/></a14:hiddenLine></a:ext></a:extLst></p:spPr><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="299804482"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide4.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/><a:t>The information roadmap shows clearly that even in the project stage it will have an impact on cost and schedule, by minimizing misunderstandings and associated risk of rework. </a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/><a:t>Complete and accurate as built information also gives the operational organization the opportunity to familiarize themselves with the infrastructure and generate product and profit quicker.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/><a:t>Prerequisites for a successful handover are:</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" b="1" dirty="0"/><a:t>Engineering Numbering Specification (procedure)</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Defining a skeleton on how information is stored and organized facilitates the communication </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>between your project stakeholders and clarifies required information needs.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" b="1" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Plant Breakdown Structure.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Document ID.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Tag structure.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" b="1" dirty="0"/><a:t>Life-cycle information Requirements Specification (Documentation for Operations)</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>What to deliver and when.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="932785"><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Remember the Information lifecycle during the project timeline, the project teams need to be able to generate their part or provide feedback if questions arise.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Operational teams need to be able to prepare themselves for receiving the as built end result. Information is needed prior the handover of the facility.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="861456"/><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>As-built tolerances, inclusion of “spare” capacity, incl. supplier scope</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>How to deliver.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="932785"><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>The operational team should define their information requirements, needed during the operational lifecycle of the infrastructure.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>What are the formats and tools (database, 3D model, 2D CAD, ) Assuring the standardization of tools/applications needed by every partner during the project stage.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" b="1" dirty="0"/><a:t>Input to Contract Scope of Work </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Ensure that contractors and suppliers understand the information requirements and allow provision to organize themselves for delivering this information.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Proper definition and organisation will facilitate follow-up during the project, planning and undertaking of audits and also validate deliverables.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" b="1" dirty="0"/><a:t>Information Manager  with responsibility for the digital asset</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Identify coordinator in EPC with responsibility for digital plant delivery (append job description)</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Early access to/handover of data and documentation </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Early review of deliverables with disciplines to ensure understanding and compliance</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/><a:t>Subject to project audit</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:rPr><a:pPr/><a:t>4</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:endParaRPr></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="1748178293"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide5.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Plant</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> Asset Information varies in the degree of intelligence it offers and the technical solutions that Intergraph offer to manage the take-on of this information </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>[CLICK]</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>At the lowest end of the spectrum there are hard copy archives and DVDs that contain scanned images. These can either be considered as left in their original formats with a reference in SPO to the physical location or hardcopy documents can be scanned and treated as unstructured data that will be discussed later </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>[CLICK]</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Non-integrated design tools, SmartPlant and others can deliver reports that can be</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> incorporated into SPO </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>[CLICK]</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Unstructured information such as scanned documents/drawings, MS Office Files and 2D CAD drawings can be processed by SmartPlant Fusion that can extract intelligence, provide hotspots and links. This information can be accesses in SP Fusion or promoted to SPO where it can be maintained. </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>[CLICK]</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>For structured information deliverables such as tag registers (Equipment lists, instrument index, cable schedules etc..), document registers and cross references Intergraph offers SDV that can validate information deliverables against sets of business rules and report errors found before data is loaded into target systems.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Where integrated SmartPlant design tools have been deployed then the SPO Handover tool is available to take sub-sets of information from one SPF based system e.g. an EPC Contractor system over to another SPF based system e.g. the Owner Operator’s SPO system. The data transferred will retain all the intelligence when installed on the target system e.g. intelligent navigation. </a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr defTabSz="903976" eaLnBrk="0" fontAlgn="base" hangingPunct="0"><a:spcBef><a:spcPct val="30000"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPct val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>These various</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> approaches ensure that in all cases Intergraph can offer appropriate solutions to take-on information for legacy facilities or from projects.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"/><a:t>5</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="1947826099"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide6.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>0-</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> So hearing what you saying, I think it is fair to say that one of the biggest challenges during</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> information handover. Is managing, assessing and validating the handover information and quality. The Handover documentation consists usually out of various parts, talking about various types of documents such as Drawings, Datasheets, Certificates, General Arrangements etc. Which are available as PDF-Files, Native Files, in Office Formats and so on. Looking at this. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>1-</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> Managing the handover of data and documentation from projects to operations is especially daunting. The volumes are vast – even a moderate-sized project can contain hundreds of thousands of tags and documents, and millions of individual data items.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr marL="0" lvl="1" defTabSz="940460"><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>2-</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> Additionally Data and documents are often delivered with missing, incorrect or inconsistent data.  But it’s up to information managers to ens</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>ure that only correct and validated data is loaded into target systems.  </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>As a rule, data handover can easily account for 1 to 2% of total CAPEX costs and take a year or more to complete. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>3- </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>As</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="0" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> we already mentioned at the beginning of this webinar</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> the real value associated with quality data handover is in the operations phase.  High-quality data is critical to the safe, reliable and effective operation of any manufacturing facility.  If the quality of information handed over to operations is questionable from the start, plant</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> personnel quickly learn that they have to walk-down equipment to </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>physically verify the true state of the plant. Of course, this dramatically increases operational costs over the entire lifecycle of the facility.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" b="1" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>4-</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> And again, although we are looking at technologies</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> and strategies today, </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>Data validation isn’t something that can be fully automated. I</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>t </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>requires input from highly competent individuals with a thorough knowledge of engineering information.  And as you know, these are often scarcest of resources.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t>So</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/><a:t> Joris, can you elaborate a bit further on the common handover challenges that are resulting out of the scenario I have described.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" dirty="0" smtClean="0"/></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"/><a:t>6</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="1235847653"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide7.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Well Sascha, every operational facility has to produce cost effectively whilst assuring the supply chain and keep manufacturing safe. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>To keep the facility up and running it is evident that we, within the organization, understand one another and communication is crucial. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>During operation it is often a challenge to monitor performance or do asset maintenance. </a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:pPr marL="0" marR="0" indent="0" algn="l" defTabSz="914400" rtl="0" eaLnBrk="1" fontAlgn="auto" latinLnBrk="0" hangingPunct="1"><a:lnSpc><a:spcPct val="100000"/></a:lnSpc><a:spcBef><a:spcPts val="0"/></a:spcBef><a:spcAft><a:spcPts val="0"/></a:spcAft><a:buClrTx/><a:buSzTx/><a:buFontTx/><a:buNone/><a:tabLst/><a:defRPr/></a:pPr><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>&lt;CLICK&gt;</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Preparation for a task or intervention is not possible because the asset information is inaccurate, out of date or simply missing in general. Technicians might </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" err="1" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>evn</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> come back with issues on-site that have not been documented in the past. The sketches, redlines created ad hoc in the field typically lead to local copies of information. That as built information or the location of it will only be available in peoples minds. One might wonder how to access that information if</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>these peoples are off duty or worse leave the organization.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Another example we all are familiar with is the preparation of a turn around. Walk downs are time and cost-intensive.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Adequate Asset Information Management can help here to reduce these activities or provide a base line to define the scope of work of a turnaround.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Another reason</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> to raise the Asset Information level is s</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>howing compliance, fulfilling all potential regularly requirements in order to operate the asset. As the risk of incidents increase this becomes even more important especially looking at the Environmental, Health and Safety aspects. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="bg1"><a:lumMod val="95000"/></a:schemeClr></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:rPr><a:pPr/><a:t>7</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"><a:solidFill><a:prstClr val="black"/></a:solidFill></a:endParaRPr></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="2088314720"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>

## File: ppt\notesSlides\notesSlide9.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<p:notes xmlns:a="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships" xmlns:p="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main"><p:cSld><p:spTree><p:nvGrpSpPr><p:cNvPr id="1" name=""/><p:cNvGrpSpPr/><p:nvPr/></p:nvGrpSpPr><p:grpSpPr><a:xfrm><a:off x="0" y="0"/><a:ext cx="0" cy="0"/><a:chOff x="0" y="0"/><a:chExt cx="0" cy="0"/></a:xfrm></p:grpSpPr><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="2" name="Slide Image Placeholder 1"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1" noRot="1" noChangeAspect="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldImg"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="3" name="Notes Placeholder 2"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="body" idx="1"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>This slide is all about handover! Mechanical completion and commissioning are the best example I can think off to show the value of available information.</a:t></a:r></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Mechanical completion and commissioning are the last steps in the project lifecycle. And the first time the engineering information is put to the test versus the physical as built equipment. </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>P&amp;ID walk downs are crucial prior wet runs. Instrumentation loop checks can become very tedious when loop diagrams or terminal layouts are incorrect.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>Potentially, even part of the engineering will</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> be </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>redone. What will you select as the required build when discrepancies</a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" baseline="0" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-GB" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t>are encountered? The physical as–is situation or the designed situation as stated in the available information. I think we all know what the situation is like when ending up in this position.</a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p><a:p><a:r><a:rPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0" smtClean="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:rPr><a:t> </a:t></a:r><a:endParaRPr lang="en-US" sz="1200" kern="1200" dirty="0"><a:solidFill><a:schemeClr val="tx1"/></a:solidFill><a:effectLst/><a:latin typeface="+mn-lt"/><a:ea typeface="+mn-ea"/><a:cs typeface="+mn-cs"/></a:endParaRPr></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp><p:sp><p:nvSpPr><p:cNvPr id="4" name="Slide Number Placeholder 3"/><p:cNvSpPr><a:spLocks noGrp="1"/></p:cNvSpPr><p:nvPr><p:ph type="sldNum" sz="quarter" idx="10"/></p:nvPr></p:nvSpPr><p:spPr/><p:txBody><a:bodyPr/><a:lstStyle/><a:p><a:fld id="{8CC8ECB3-3810-4E33-9FCA-8C107FEA4094}" type="slidenum"><a:rPr lang="en-GB" smtClean="0"/><a:t>10</a:t></a:fld><a:endParaRPr lang="en-GB" dirty="0"/></a:p></p:txBody></p:sp></p:spTree><p:extLst><p:ext uri="{BB962C8B-B14F-4D97-AF65-F5344CB8AC3E}"><p14:creationId xmlns:p14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/powerpoint/2010/main" val="2129856626"/></p:ext></p:extLst></p:cSld><p:clrMapOvr><a:masterClrMapping/></p:clrMapOvr></p:notes>
